instruction: Configure auto screen lock for security: enable screen lock, set idle delay to 5 minutes, and set lock delay to 0 (immediate lock after blank). Also save a report to ~/Desktop/lock_policy.txt with exactly three lines: lock-enabled=true, idle-delay=300, lock-delay=0.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Automatically lock your screen (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/privacy-screen-lock.html.en)

parameters.command: gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled false; gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0; gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay 60; rm -f ~/Desktop/lock_policy.txt

result.command:
le=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled 2>/dev/null); id_raw=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 2>/dev/null); id_val=$(echo "$id_raw" | grep -oE '[0-9]+'); ld_raw=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay 2>/dev/null); ld_val=$(echo "$ld_raw" | grep -oE '[0-9]+'); expected=$(printf 'lock-enabled=true
idle-delay=300
lock-delay=0
'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/lock_policy.txt 2>/dev/null); file_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && file_ok='true'; echo "lock_enabled=$le"; echo "idle_delay=$id_val"; echo "lock_delay=$ld_val"; echo "file_ok=$file_ok"
